blocks.body: Time and Place Changes explains how words such as now, today, here, or this often change in reported speech.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In reported speech, we often change not only the tense but also words that show time, place, or distance from the speaker.","rules":[{"label":"Time words","color":"emerald","text":"Words such as now, today, tomorrow, and yesterday often change when the reference point changes.","example":"today → that day / tomorrow → the next day"},{"label":"Place words","color":"blue","text":"Words like here often become there if the speaker is no longer in the same place.","example":"here → there"},{"label":"This / these","color":"amber","text":"Demonstratives may also move further away: this → that, these → those.","example":"these books → those books"}]}

blocks.heading: 1. The most common changes
blocks.body:
Direct wordReported wordExample
nowthen“I am busy now.” → She said that she was busy then.
todaythat day“I have a test today.” → He said that he had a test that day.
tomorrowthe next day“We will leave tomorrow.” → They said that they would leave the next day.
yesterdaythe day before“I saw her yesterday.” → He said that he had seen her the day before.
herethere“It is cold here.” → She said that it was cold there.
this / thesethat / those“These shoes are mine.” → He said that those shoes were his.

blocks.heading: 2. When the changes are really needed
blocks.body:
• These words change when the reference point changes between the direct sentence and the report.
  • If the place, time, or situation is still the same, some words may stay unchanged.
  • The key question is: from where is the speaker looking now and when is the report happening?
  • Time words often work together with backshift, so it helps to check both levels at the same time.

blocks.heading: 3. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: keeping tomorrow when the report happens on the following day.
    Better: the next day.
  • Mistake: changing every word automatically. Sometimes today or here can stay if the context has not changed.
  • Mistake: changing the tense but forgetting this / these when the speaker is no longer close to those things.

blocks.heading: 4. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• Check the time of reporting and the speaker's position before changing words of time and place.
  • The most common pairs are now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, here → there, and this → that.
  • Do not change these words mechanically; follow the real context.
